title: Astronomy & Space
description: Planets, space missions, constellations, and famous astronomers

cards.front: What is the 1st planet from the Sun?
What is the 2nd planet from the Sun?
What is the 3rd planet from the Sun?
What is the 4th planet from the Sun?
What is the 5th planet from the Sun?
What is the 6th planet from the Sun?
What is the 7th planet from the Sun?
What is the 8th planet from the Sun?
Which planet is the largest in our solar system?
Which planet is known for its Great Red Spot?
Which planet is known for its prominent ring system?
Which planet rotates on its side?
Europa is a moon of which planet?
cards.back: Jupiter — Europa has a subsurface ocean that may support life

cards.front: Titan is a moon of which planet?
cards.back: Saturn — Titan has a thick atmosphere and liquid methane lakes

cards.front: The largest moon in the solar system
The most volcanically active body in the solar system
In what year was Pluto reclassified from planet to dwarf planet?
Which dwarf planet is located in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter?
Apollo 11
cards.back: First crewed mission to land on the Moon (July 20, 1969)

cards.front: Apollo 13
cards.back: 1970 Moon mission that suffered an oxygen tank explosion; crew returned safely (called a 'successful failure')

cards.front: NASA's first human spaceflight program (1958–1963), all single-astronaut missions
NASA program between Mercury and Apollo that practiced spacewalks and orbital rendezvous
Space shuttle that broke apart 73 seconds after launch on January 28, 1986
Space shuttle that disintegrated during reentry on February 1, 2003
The farthest human-made object from Earth
Hubble Space Telescope
cards.back: Space telescope launched in 1990 that orbits Earth and has provided iconic deep-space images

cards.front: James Webb Space Telescope
cards.back: Infrared space telescope launched in 2021; successor to Hubble, orbits the Sun near the L2 point

cards.front: NASA Mars rover that landed in 2012 in Gale Crater
NASA Mars rover that landed in 2021 and carried the Ingenuity helicopter
The first artificial satellite ever launched into orbit
First person to walk on the Moon
Neil Armstrong
cards.back: First person to walk on the Moon (Apollo 11, 1969)

cards.front: Second person to walk on the Moon, alongside Neil Armstrong on Apollo 11
First American to orbit the Earth (1962)
First American woman in space (1983)
First human in space (1961)
First American in space (1961)
First African American woman in space (1992)
Galileo Galilei
cards.back: Italian astronomer who used a telescope to observe Jupiter's moons, Saturn's rings, and phases of Venus; supported heliocentrism

cards.front: Italian astronomer who used a telescope to discover Jupiter's four largest moons
Nicolaus Copernicus
cards.back: Polish astronomer who proposed the heliocentric model — that Earth and planets revolve around the Sun

cards.front: Astronomer who proposed the heliocentric (Sun-centered) model of the solar system
Johannes Kepler
cards.back: German astronomer who discovered three laws of planetary motion, including that orbits are ellipses

cards.front: Edwin Hubble
cards.back: American astronomer who proved galaxies exist beyond the Milky Way and that the universe is expanding

cards.front: Danish astronomer known for precise pre-telescope observations; his data helped Kepler
Carl Sagan
cards.back: Astronomer and science communicator; hosted Cosmos; worked on the Voyager Golden Record

cards.front: A light year is a measure of what?
cards.back: Distance — the distance light travels in one year (about 5.88 trillion miles)

cards.front: The average distance from the Earth to the Sun (about 93 million miles)
A region of space where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape
A large cloud of gas and dust in space, often a birthplace of new stars
The explosion of a massive star at the end of its life, briefly outshining an entire galaxy
A large, cool star near the end of its life; the Sun will become one in about 5 billion years
The small, dense remnant of a low-mass star after it sheds its outer layers
The stage of a star's life in which it fuses hydrogen into helium; our Sun is in this stage
The prevailing theory for the origin of the universe, approximately 13.8 billion years ago
The constellation known as 'The Hunter,' featuring Betelgeuse and Rigel
The constellation that contains the Big Dipper asterism
The constellation that contains the Little Dipper and Polaris
A W-shaped constellation in the northern sky named after a queen in Greek mythology
Polaris
cards.back: The North Star; located at the tip of the Little Dipper (Ursa Minor); used for celestial navigation

cards.front: The current North Star
